response:
THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED AS FOLLOWS:  
THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM, AND EACH NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS.  
THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², SO THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² + π(1.1R)² + π(1.21R)² + ... + π(1.771561R)².  
THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH A COMMON RATIO OF 1.1 AND 8 TERMS.  
THE SUM OF A GEOMETRIC SERIES IS (a₁(1 - rⁿ)) / (1 - r), WHERE a₁ IS THE FIRST TERM, r IS THE COMMON RATIO, AND n IS THE NUMBER OF TERMS.  
HERE, a₁ = πR², r = 1.1, n = 8.  
THE TOTAL AREA IS πR² * (1 - 1.1⁸) / (1 - 1.1).  
THIS IS THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.  

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS.  
THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2π(1.1⁴R) = 2π(1.4641R).  
THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS IS 0.5 CM, SO THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5.  
THIS IS 2π(1.4641R) / 0.5 = 4π(1.4641R).  
THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE.
